diagnosis.final_diagnosis: Subarachnoid hemorrhage with anterior communicating artery aneurysm, right frontal cerebrovascular accident, right eye blindness and left eye visual field cut, right basal ganglia infarct, urinary tract infection
diagnosis.diagnostic_reasoning: The diagnosis of subarachnoid hemorrhage (SAH) was established based on the patient’s presentation with severe headache, loss of consciousness, and diagnostic imaging findings including CT and CTA that confirmed SAH and an aneurysm of the anterior communicating artery. The patient's subsequent vision loss was attributed to the compressive effect of the aneurysm on the optic chiasm.
